text:
Market today: The mood is mixed. Nvidia's AI news is a strong positive for your chip names, but concerns around private tech valuations (SpaceX) could temper overall tech sentiment.

Your holdings: SOXL (-44.1%) and SPAL (-39.92%) are your biggest headaches, both leveraged and decaying. SMH (-10.55%) also needs attention. Remember, margin amplifies these drops.

Watch: Keep a tight eye on SOXL's stop; it's a leveraged product that decays fast. Also, watch for follow-through on Nvidia's AI news for the broader chip sector.

Bottom line: Aggressively manage your leveraged losers while watching for confirmed strength in core chip names.
plays: SOXL: Your stop at 200 is too loose given the -44.1% loss. Tighten it significantly or trim this leveraged position to reduce decay risk.
SMH: Trim this position if it breaks its 540 stop. Protecting capital on this -10.55% loss is key.
No new buys today - hold and watch. While Nvidia news is positive for chips, no scanner setups offer a compelling, low-risk new entry right now.
